title: 有翼之暗
summary:
Mutsu-tsune discusses the "Book of Raknawa" (also referred to as the "Le Cenacol Book"), an occult scripture published in Japan thirty years ago that serves as a defamation of Jesus Christ. The text posits a "Jesus twin" theory, suggesting that Mary gave birth to twins and that Jesus used teleportation to appear in two places. According to the book, the figure crucified at Golgotha was the brother, while the elder brother intended to resurrect to establish Christianity; the text further portrays Judas as a faithful servant who was killed to ensure silence. Mutsu-tsune reveals that a rose-patterned washi paper bookmark was left inside the book, which he believes was left by the murderer.

This scripture connects to a monochrome woodcut painting in Kaname's room that depicts the crucifixion of two Jesus twins. Mutsu-tsune suggests that both the book and the painting serve as metaphors for the murders of Ito and Uma, indicating the killer's motive involves a "rebellion against God."

Providing a final explanation to detectives Motegi and Horikawa, Mutsu-tsune presents a hypothesis regarding the underlying phenomena of the case. He notes that while decapitation is the surface phenomenon, the killer's behavior is distinctive because the severed heads and limbs were not hidden. Mutsu-tsune argues that if the killer's goal were a "replacement trick," the body parts would have been concealed. Instead, the decision to leave the parts intact and discoverable suggests that decapitation itself is the direct intention rather than a means to hide an identity.
